subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **100** numbered cards (1-100)
- **Life cards** (number of lives)
- **Throwing Star cards** (number of throwing stars)
- **Level cards** (1-12)

subtopics.title: Table Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Shuffle numbered cards** (1-100).
2. **Place Level 1 card** in center.
3. **Lives** — Start with lives equal to player count.
4. **Throwing Stars** — Start with 1.
5. **Rewards** — Certain levels grant bonus lives or throwing stars.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Level 1** — Each player receives 1 card.
- **Level 2** — Each player receives 2 cards, etc.
- **No communication** — You may NOT discuss or signal about your cards.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: The Mind is a cooperative card game where players must play their numbered cards in ascending order without communicating. Develop a telepathic sense of timing with your teammates to succeed.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
There are no turns! All players play simultaneously:

1. **Focus** — Players place a hand on the table to signal they're ready.
2. **Play cards** — When you feel the time is right, play your lowest card face up on the center pile.
3. **Ascending order** — Cards must be played in ascending numerical order across all players.
4. **Continue** — Keep playing until all cards for the level are played.

There is no turn order. Anyone can play a card at any time.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Playing Cards
- Each player plays their own cards when they feel the moment is right.
- Cards must go on the pile in ascending order.
- If someone plays a card and another player has a lower card they haven't played, that's an error.

#### Errors
- When a card is played out of order, any player(s) with lower unplayed cards must discard them.
- The team loses 1 life per error.
- If lives reach 0, the game is lost.

#### Throwing Stars
- Any player can propose using a throwing star by raising their hand.
- If all players agree, each player discards their lowest card face up.
- This gives everyone information and clears potential conflicts.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **No communication** — No talking, no signals, no gestures about your cards.
- **Concentration gesture** — Placing hand on table only means "I'm focused/ready."
- **Rewards** — After completing certain levels, gain a life or throwing star.
- **Blind mode** (variant) — Play cards face down for an extreme challenge.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Win
- Complete all levels (Level 12 in a 2-player game, Level 10 in 3-player, Level 8 in 4-player).

#### Lose
- Lose all lives.

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Wait longer for lower numbers** — If you have a card like 15, wait a bit. If you have 85, wait a lot.
- **Develop rhythm** — The more you play together, the better your group timing becomes.
- **Use throwing stars wisely** — When someone looks anxious, a throwing star can resolve conflict.
- **Trust the pause** — Long pauses usually mean someone has a tricky middle number.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Mental clock** — Mentally count seconds — roughly 1 second per number to gauge timing.
- **Read body language** — While you can't signal about cards, natural reactions give clues.
- **Throwing star timing** — Use stars when you have cards close together (e.g., 41 and 43).
- **Early cards go fast** — Cards 1-10 should be played almost immediately.
- **Experience matters** — The Mind gets easier the more you play with the same group.
